summary:
Modern bone sounding primarily utilizes advanced imaging modalities like cone beam computed tomography rather than direct physical probing or other invasive procedures due to its superior accuracy and reduced risk compared to historical approaches. The shift reflects a broader trend toward minimally invasive yet comprehensive assessment tools across various fields within orthopedics and dentistry today.
 To refine this summary further while ensuring it adheres strictly only to conveying critical information:

Modern bone assessments often use Cone Beam Computed Tomography instead of traditional tapping methods because these newer scans provide detailed images without needing touch-based checks. This transition highlights advancements favoring safer, more precise diagnosis techniques currently favored in medicine. Sentence count maintained at two concise statements focusing solely on essential details pertinent to answering how contemporary practitioners perform 'sounding' bones. Agent
To summarize effectively based directly upon your instructions:

Cone Beam Computed Tomography has replaced traditional bone-sounding methods in modern practice due to its ability to provide detailed imagery without using manual probes. This approach aligns with recent technological improvements aimed at minimizing patient discomfort and improving diagnostic precision.
